ppm_edge_cubed_sphere.accuracy: O(h^2) at panel-boundary interfaces (deliberate fallback from the interior O(h^4) PPM edge formula)
ppm_edge_cubed_sphere.reference: Harris et al. (2021), GFDL FV3 Technical Memorandum, Eq. 6.5–6.6.
ppm_edge_cubed_sphere.notes: Two-sided PPM extrapolation at cubed-sphere panel-boundary interfaces. The standard 4th-order PPM edge formula (CW84 eq. 1.6, encoded in finite_volume/ppm_reconstruction.json) is inaccurate across the discontinuous panel coordinate system; this rule averages two one-sided extrapolations, one from each side of the boundary. The stencil samples cells at offsets {-1, 0, 1, 2} along the chosen axis ($x ∈ {xi, eta}) and produces the value at the panel-edge interface lying between offsets 0 and 1; cross-panel offsets are resolved to neighbor-panel ghost cells (with the appropriate 2×2 basis rotation) by the cubed_sphere grid accessor (src/grids/panel_connectivity.jl), so selectors carry no `panel` field per SELECTOR_KINDS.md decision #13. On the gnomonic equidistant cubed sphere the spacing is uniform isotropic h = dξ = dη = π/(2·Nc), so the FV3 non-uniform formula a_E = (1/2)·[(2 dx_1 + dx_2) q_1 − dx_1 q_2] / (dx_1 + dx_2) + (1/2)·[(2 dx_0 + dx_{-1}) q_0 − dx_0 q_{-1}] / (dx_0 + dx_{-1}) collapses to the 4-cell linear stencil with coefficients [-1/4, 3/4, 3/4, -1/4]. The monotonicity constraint of FV3 eq. 6.6 (clamp the linear value to the [min, max] range of the four contributing cells) is documented separately under `monotonicity_constraint` and is not part of the linear stencil — encoding a post-stencil clamp in the ESS §7 schema is a follow-up.
ppm_edge_cubed_sphere.panel_handling: lives in the cubed_sphere grid accessor (src/grids/panel_connectivity.jl); the rule references in-panel offsets only.

metric_bindings.comment: The two-sided edge formula on a uniform isotropic gnomonic equidistant grid carries no metric coefficients — all `dx_k = h` cancel from the FV3 non-uniform formula. The per-axis spacing symbol `h` is reported here for cross-reference with the cubed_sphere accessor (matches src/grids/cubed_sphere.jl `dξ` / `dη` fields, both equal to π/(2·Nc)).
metric_bindings.h: uniform isotropic spacing dξ = dη = π / (2 Nc)

edge_value_stencil.comment: FV3 eq. 6.5 (two-sided extrapolation) specialized to uniform isotropic spacing on the gnomonic equidistant cubed sphere. The 4-point stencil over offsets {-1, 0, 1, 2} carries coefficients [-1/4, 3/4, 3/4, -1/4]; the value sits on the cell-edge interface between cells at offsets 0 and 1. Compare with the standard interior 4th-order PPM edge formula (CW84 eq. 1.6) [-1/12, 7/12, 7/12, -1/12]: same 4 cells, different weights — the two-sided form is exact for linear fields only, by design at panel boundaries where the higher-order formula breaks down.

monotonicity_constraint.comment: FV3 eq. 6.6: after the linear stencil produces a_E, clamp it to the closed range [min(q_{-1}, q_0, q_1, q_2), max(q_{-1}, q_0, q_1, q_2)] of the four contributing cell means, preventing new extrema from forming at panel-boundary interfaces. This is a non-linear post-processing step expressed against the same stencil cells; the rule carries the linear stencil only because the ESS §7 schema does not yet encode post-stencil clamps. Bindings that need the limited form apply `clamp(a_E, qmin, qmax)` after the linear sum.
monotonicity_constraint.follow_up: ESS extension to encode post-stencil clamp guards (parallel to the `formula` AST in flux_limiter_minmod.json) is tracked separately.
